test.note: Known flaky test (config::tests::test_five_layer_precedence_ordering) fails only when run in parallel due to environmental pollution - passes with --test-threads=1. This is a test isolation issue, not an implementation issue, documented in prior M12 and M10 validations.

blockingIssues.description: GitTool::create() factory method exists (ecbf844) and properly injects SecretScanner, but no production code path uses it. All callers (in tests and production) still use GitTool::new() which initializes secret_scanner: None. The scan_staged() call in git_commit() remains unreachable in production because the scanner is never injected. The security contract 'commit is blocked when secrets are detected' cannot be fulfilled.
blockingIssues.evidence: Commit ecbf844 adds GitTool::create() factory, but grep across codebase shows all GitTool instantiations use GitTool::new() - no callers use GitTool::create(). The fix creates the factory but doesn't migrate any call sites.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance that Optional<T> fields initialized to None in constructors require explicit evidence that the optional dependency is actually injected at some production call site. Having `if let Some(scanner) = self.scanner { ... }` is not sufficient - the code path that injects the scanner must exist AND be verified via grep that actual callers use the injection point. Workers must grep for usages of builder/factory methods, not just verify the builder exists.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: safety-secret-scanning review: GitTool::create() exists and correctly injects SecretScanner, but grep shows no callers use it. All GitTool::new() calls remain unchanged. This systemic pattern ('builder exists but unwired') repeats across multiple features. Prior round 2 synthesis flagged this same issue.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance that security features requiring middleware injection must document all call sites that need updating. Workers must migrate ALL call sites, not just add builder methods. A feature is complete only when all production call sites are migrated to use the secure factory/builder.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: safety-secret-scanning: only 1 call site needed migration but it was missed. The fix added GitTool::create() but didn't update the actual GitTool::new() call in the production code path.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that 'rust-worker' skill referenced in features.json does not exist. Workers should use the 'rust' skill instead. This was flagged in prior synthesis but the non-existent skill reference persists in features.json.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple M12 features reference 'rust-worker' skill which does not exist. The valid skill is 'rust'.

rejectedObservations.observation: safety-resource-limits has incomplete circuit breaker implementation
rejectedObservations.reason: The feature description mentions circuit breaker pattern, but SessionResourceTracker uses simple failure counting which is functionally equivalent. The swell-core CircuitBreaker exists but is not required for the feature's core behavior. The round 2 review flagged this as blocking but the implementation meets the functional requirements of max_turns, max_cost_usd, and max_consecutive_failures limits. Commit 2ac857e correctly wires record_turn(), record_cost(), and record_success().
